figures.caption: FIG. 2. Four anode chemistries placed onto the stable branch of the saddle-node closure at 𝛼 = 0.05. Markers: graphite (blue circle), silicon composite (green square), lithium metal (red triangle), anode-free Li/Cu (purple diamond). Anodefree sits at 𝐾/𝐾 ≈ 0.95, within 5% of the critical point. The ordering is preserved across 𝛼 ∈ [0.01,0.5].

FIG. 3. (a) CE-residual autocorrelation length 𝜉 vs cycle number for the four chemistries (continuous 1/𝑒 crossing, 30- cycle sliding window). (b) Theoretical critical-slowing-down exponent 𝜏 ∝ (𝐾 − 𝐾) / from Eq. (10), with each chemistry placed at its measured 𝐾/𝐾 .
FIG. 4. (a) Critical current ratio 𝑗 /𝑗 vs 𝐾/𝐾 for 𝑝 = 1,2,3. Anode-free at 𝐾/𝐾 = 0.954 marked. (b) Critical temperature shift 𝛥𝑇 vs 𝐾/𝐾 for 𝐸 = 0.4 eV, 𝑇 = 298 K.
FIG. 1. (a) Saddle-node bifurcation of the closure 𝑑𝑢/𝑑𝜏 = 𝐾 − 𝑢/(1 + 𝛼𝑢 ) for 𝛼 = 1. Solid: stable branch.
